name: Cetirizine
mechanism: Selective peripheral H1 receptor antagonist
evidence_notes: First-line MCAS rescue; reduces histamine-driven urticaria, pruritus, rhinorrhea.
source_refs: Afrin LB et al. 2020 MCAS consensus-2

name: Loratadine
mechanism: Selective peripheral H1 receptor antagonist
evidence_notes: Non-sedating alternative; common in MCAS step-1 protocols.

name: Fexofenadine
mechanism: Selective peripheral H1 receptor antagonist
evidence_notes: Often used at 2-4x standard dose in MCAS per consensus. Large (502 Da), zwitterionic, P-glycoprotein substrate — by design peripherally-restricted. Oral only; buccal/sublingual route NOT expected to give fast onset.

name: Hydroxyzine
mechanism: H1 antagonist + anxiolytic; crosses BBB
evidence_notes: Used for sleep + neuro symptoms in MCAS.

name: Diphenhydramine
mechanism: H1 antagonist; rescue route for anaphylaxis
evidence_notes: Acute rescue; sedating. Small (255 Da), lipophilic (logP 3.3), single tertiary amine — chemistry strongly favors buccal / sublingual / IM rescue with fast onset (5-15 min).

name: Famotidine
mechanism: H2 receptor antagonist
evidence_notes: Standard with H1 in MCAS; GI + systemic histamine.

name: Ranitidine
mechanism: H2 receptor antagonist
evidence_notes: Withdrawn 2020 (NDMA impurity). Included for SAR/reference only.

name: Cromolyn sodium
mechanism: Inhibits mast cell degranulation; reduces Ca2+ influx
evidence_notes: Oral (Gastrocrom) for GI MCAS; poorly absorbed systemically. Disodium salt makes it incompatible with buccal absorption.

name: Ketotifen
mechanism: H1 antagonist + mast cell stabilizer
evidence_notes: Off-label in US; widely used by MCAS clinicians.

name: Ascorbic acid
mechanism: Accelerates histamine degradation; antioxidant; cofactor for DAO
evidence_notes: Adjunct; supports DAO activity.

name: Quercetin
mechanism: Inhibits PKC, Ca2+ influx, histamine + tryptase + cytokine release
evidence_notes: Outperforms cromolyn in human mast cell assays for some readouts.
source_refs: Weng Z et al. 2012 PLOS ONE; Theoharides 2015

name: Bromelain
mechanism: Reduces allergic inflammation; possible histamine modulation
evidence_notes: Adjunct in allergic rhinitis; mechanism not fully resolved.

name: Stinging nettle leaf
mechanism: Natural H1 antagonism + COX/LOX inhibition; multi-component
evidence_notes: Standardized extracts (Urtica dioica) studied in allergic rhinitis.

name: Diamine oxidase (DAO)
mechanism: Degrades dietary histamine in the gut lumen pre-absorption
evidence_notes: Taken before high-histamine meals; useful in histamine intolerance subset.

name: Montelukast
mechanism: CysLT1 receptor antagonist; blocks LTD4/LTE4
evidence_notes: Standard MCAS maintenance; respiratory + GI symptoms.

name: Naltrexone (low-dose)
mechanism: TLR4 antagonism; glial / immune modulation at low dose
evidence_notes: Anecdotal in MCAS, fibromyalgia, autoimmune; off-label.

name: Luteolin
mechanism: Inhibits PKC, NF-kB, mast cell + microglia activation; crosses BBB
evidence_notes: Stronger than quercetin in several neuro-mast-cell assays.

name: Curcumin
mechanism: NF-kB inhibition; Nrf2 activation; antioxidant
evidence_notes: Phytosomal forms (Meriva) improve bioavailability.

name: EGCG
mechanism: Mast cell stabilization; antioxidant; Nrf2 activator
evidence_notes: Polyphenol with broad anti-allergic effects.

name: Resveratrol
mechanism: Sirtuin / Nrf2 activator; MRGPRX2 inhibition
evidence_notes: Inhibits non-IgE pseudo-allergic activation.

name: Silibinin
mechanism: Hepatoprotective; antioxidant; mast cell modulation
evidence_notes: Active component of silymarin complex.

name: Apigenin
mechanism: Mast cell stabilizer; NF-kB inhibition
evidence_notes: Synergizes with quercetin/luteolin.

name: Kaempferol
mechanism: Antioxidant; anti-inflammatory; mast cell modulation
evidence_notes: Common dietary flavonoid; broad signaling effects.

name: Hesperidin
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; capillary stabilization
evidence_notes: Studied in allergic rhinitis.

name: Rutin
mechanism: Antioxidant; histamine release inhibition
evidence_notes: Quercetin-3-rutinoside; lower bioavailability.

name: Myricitrin
mechanism: Antioxidant; anti-allergic
evidence_notes: Less-studied flavonoid worth SAR exploration.

name: Cholecalciferol (Vitamin D3)
mechanism: Immunomodulation; mast cell stability; T-reg support
evidence_notes: Deficiency common in MCAS; correction reduces flare frequency.

name: Calcitriol
mechanism: VDR agonist; downregulates mast cell IgE response
evidence_notes: Active hormone; clinical use mainly in renal disease.

name: Docosahexaenoic acid (DHA)
mechanism: SPM precursor; anti-inflammatory; membrane modulation
evidence_notes: Reduces systemic inflammation; supports membrane stability.

name: Eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA)
mechanism: SPM precursor; reduces LTB4; mast cell modulation
evidence_notes: Co-administered with DHA in fish oil.

name: Butyric acid
mechanism: HDAC inhibition; gut barrier reinforcement; Treg induction
evidence_notes: Microbiome-derived; supports gut-mast axis.

name: N-Acetylcysteine (NAC)
mechanism: GSH replenishment; ROS scavenging; Nrf2 activation (indirect)
evidence_notes: Supports oxidative-stress reduction upstream.

name: Taurine
mechanism: Membrane stabilization; osmoregulation; anti-inflammatory
evidence_notes: Down-regulates degranulation in some assays.

name: Alpha-lipoic acid
mechanism: Recycles GSH/Vit C/E; Nrf2 activator
evidence_notes: Neuroprotective; mitochondrial support.

name: Biotin
mechanism: Cofactor for carboxylases; downregulates degranulation (animal data)
evidence_notes: Adjunct only; weak direct evidence.

name: Baicalein
mechanism: Potent mast cell stabilizer; 12-LOX inhibitor
evidence_notes: Strong inhibition of histamine + leukotriene release.

name: Rosmarinic acid
mechanism: Anti-allergic; mast cell stabilization
evidence_notes: Studied in seasonal allergy trials.

name: Thymoquinone
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; histamine pathway inhibition
evidence_notes: Active component of black cumin seed oil.

name: 6-Gingerol
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; prostaglandin inhibition; mast cell stability
evidence_notes: Common GI-supportive natural.

name: Aspirin (acetylsalicylic acid)
mechanism: COX-1/2 inhibition; PGD2 suppression
evidence_notes: Used in prostaglandin-high MCAS subset under clinical supervision (NSAID-tolerant only).

name: Masitinib
mechanism: KIT, LYN, FYN inhibition
evidence_notes: Phase 3 trials for severe indolent systemic mastocytosis / severe MCAS.

name: Sulforaphane
mechanism: Covalent KEAP1 modifier (Cys-151/273/288 isothiocyanate adduct) -> Nrf2 stabilization + nuclear translocation; HO-1 / NQO1 induction; NF-kB / caspase-1 inhibition; epigenetic (DNMT/HDAC) modulation
evidence_notes: Upstream rescue candidate for oxidative + epigenetic 'injury'; suppresses mast cell degranulation in vitro. Small (177 Da), modestly lipophilic — absorbs sublingually from chewed broccoli sprouts (myrosinase activation in mouth).

name: Glucoraphanin
mechanism: Converted to sulforaphane by myrosinase; same downstream Nrf2 activation
evidence_notes: Bioavailability depends on co-administered myrosinase or gut bacteria.

name: Semaglutide
mechanism: GLP-1R agonism; broad anti-inflammatory; possible direct mast cell stabilization
evidence_notes: 2025 refractory-MCAS case series: ~89% response. Peptide — no canonical small-molecule SMILES.

name: Tirzepatide
mechanism: Dual GLP-1R + GIPR agonism; anti-inflammatory; metabolic stabilization
evidence_notes: Same 2025 case series cohort. Peptide.

name: Omalizumab
mechanism: Binds free IgE; downregulates FcεRI surface expression on mast cells/basophils
evidence_notes: Approved for chronic spontaneous urticaria; off-label in MCAS. Antibody — no SMILES.

name: Avapritinib
mechanism: Selective KIT D816V tyrosine kinase inhibition
evidence_notes: FDA-approved for advanced systemic mastocytosis (2021).

name: Iberin
mechanism: Covalent KEAP1 modifier (Cys); Nrf2 activator; shorter-chain SFN analog
evidence_notes: Found in cabbage, broccoli. Same warhead as SFN; one CH2 shorter.

name: Erucin
mechanism: Sulfide form of SFN; reduced + bioequivalent in vivo via S-oxidation; covalent KEAP1 modifier
evidence_notes: Found in arugula / Eruca sativa. Plasma half-life longer than SFN; interconverts in vivo.

name: Sulforaphene
mechanism: Covalent KEAP1 modifier; unsaturated SFN homolog with potentially stronger Nrf2 activation
evidence_notes: From radish seeds (Raphanus sativus). C2=C3 double bond vs SFN.

name: Allyl isothiocyanate
mechanism: Covalent KEAP1 modifier; TRPA1 agonist (pungent)
evidence_notes: From mustard / horseradish / wasabi. Smaller and more volatile than SFN.

name: Benzyl isothiocyanate
mechanism: Covalent KEAP1 modifier; broader cytotoxic profile
evidence_notes: From garden cress, papaya seeds. Aromatic distal anchor; explores another pharmacophore class.

name: Phenethyl isothiocyanate
mechanism: Covalent KEAP1 modifier; HDAC inhibition; well-studied chemopreventive
evidence_notes: From watercress (Nasturtium officinale). Strong Nrf2 + epigenetic activity.

name: Midostaurin
mechanism: KIT, FLT3, PKC inhibition
evidence_notes: Approved for advanced systemic mastocytosis.

name: Cannabidiol
mechanism: CB2 weak agonist + TRPV1/TRPA1 modulator + 5-HT1A partial agonist; mast-cell stabilizer in vitro; PPAR-gamma; some Nrf2 induction
evidence_notes: Multiple in vitro studies show inhibition of mast-cell degranulation; clinical anti-inflammatory data in epilepsy + atopic conditions. CBD-rich extracts anecdotally reported in MCAS communities.

name: Cannabigerol
mechanism: CB1/CB2 weak partial agonist; alpha-2 adrenergic agonist; 5-HT1A antagonist; anti-inflammatory
evidence_notes: Parent cannabinoid; less mast-cell data than CBD but anti-inflammatory profile + emerging interest in IBD/skin conditions.

name: Cannabichromene
mechanism: TRPA1 agonist; endocannabinoid-system modulator; anti-inflammatory
evidence_notes: Less-studied minor cannabinoid; preclinical anti-inflammatory + analgesic effects.

name: Cannabinol
mechanism: CB2-biased partial agonist; sedative
evidence_notes: Forms as THC oxidizes; CB2-biased profile of interest for peripheral immune modulation.

name: Delta-9-THC
subcategory: Phytocannabinoid (intoxicating; controlled substance, US Schedule I as of pre-2024 status)
mechanism: CB1/CB2 partial agonist; mast-cell-stabilizing in vitro via CB2
evidence_notes: Bisogno 1997 / Facci 1995 showed THC inhibits mast-cell degranulation in vitro. Regulatory caveat: Schedule I in US; DEA rescheduling under review. Listed for in-silico scoring only.

name: Delta-8-THC
subcategory: Phytocannabinoid (intoxicating but less potent than delta-9)
mechanism: CB1/CB2 agonist; biased toward CB2 vs delta-9
evidence_notes: Position isomer of delta-9-THC. Federal hemp-derived legal status in US under 2018 Farm Bill; rapidly evolving.

name: Tetrahydrocannabivarin
mechanism: CB1 neutral antagonist + CB2 partial agonist (dose-dependent)
evidence_notes: Propyl side chain in place of THC's pentyl. Anti-inflammatory + appetite suppressant profile in preclinical work.

name: Cannabidivarin
mechanism: TRPA1/TRPV1 modulator; CB1/CB2 weak affinity; anti-inflammatory
evidence_notes: Non-intoxicating CBD analog. Some seizure trials. Less mast-cell data than CBD.

name: Palmitoylethanolamide
mechanism: PPAR-alpha agonist + indirect endocannabinoid 'entourage' (raises AEA tone) + direct mast-cell stabilization (ALIA pathway, Levi-Montalcini coinage)
evidence_notes: Strongest non-rx natural-product mast-cell-stabilizer evidence base. ~30 clinical trials in pain, atopy, IBD. Used as ultra-micronized formulation (Normast, PeaPure). Safe; no abuse potential.

name: Oleoylethanolamide
mechanism: PPAR-alpha agonist; anti-inflammatory + satiety signal
evidence_notes: PEA's mono-unsaturated congener. Less direct mast-cell data; included for class completeness.

name: Anandamide
mechanism: CB1/CB2 partial agonist + TRPV1; endogenous endocannabinoid
evidence_notes: Endogenous; pharmacological supplementation is impractical (rapid FAAH degradation). Scored for class context; therapeutic targeting is via FAAH inhibitors (not in library).

name: 2-Arachidonoylglycerol
mechanism: CB1/CB2 full agonist; major endocannabinoid signal
evidence_notes: Endogenous; rapid MAGL degradation; supplementation impractical. Class anchor.

name: Beta-caryophyllene
mechanism: Selective CB2 agonist (Ki ~155 nM) — unique among dietary terpenes; mast-cell stabilizer; anti-inflammatory in colitis/arthritis models
evidence_notes: Found in black pepper, cloves, hops, cannabis. FDA GRAS food additive — only CB2 agonist with that status. Strongest single-terpene case for MCAS-axis activity.

name: Alpha-humulene
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory (TNF-alpha / IL-1beta suppression); analgesic
evidence_notes: Co-occurs with beta-caryophyllene; weaker CB2 affinity than caryophyllene but anti-inflammatory in airway + colitis models.

name: Alpha-pinene
mechanism: Bronchodilator; acetylcholinesterase inhibitor (mild); anti-inflammatory
evidence_notes: Pine, rosemary, conifers. Bronchodilator profile of interest for respiratory MCAS phenotype. Also a documented MCAS VOC trigger for some individuals — bidirectional.

name: Beta-pinene
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; anti-microbial; bronchodilator
evidence_notes: Position isomer of alpha-pinene. Similar bidirectional VOC behavior in MCAS.

name: D-Limonene
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; GSH induction; mast-cell stabilization (LAD2 in vitro)
evidence_notes: Citrus peel oil. Hirota 2010 showed inhibition of mast-cell-mediated allergic inflammation. Also a top-tier MCAS VOC trigger in fragrance reports — strongly bidirectional.

name: Linalool
mechanism: Anxiolytic + anti-inflammatory + mast-cell stabilizer in vitro
evidence_notes: Lavender, basil, coriander. Kim 1999 showed inhibition of compound 48/80 mast-cell activation. Also documented MCAS fragrance trigger.

name: Beta-myrcene
mechanism: Sedative; analgesic; anti-inflammatory; weak mu-opioid potentiator
evidence_notes: Hops, cannabis, mango. Sedative + muscle-relaxant profile.

name: Eucalyptol
mechanism: Mucolytic; anti-inflammatory (NF-kB suppression); bronchodilator
evidence_notes: Eucalyptus, rosemary, tea tree. Approved as inhaled mucolytic in EU (Soledum). Bidirectional: also respiratory trigger for some individuals.

name: Terpinolene
mechanism: Antioxidant; sedative; mild anti-inflammatory
evidence_notes: Cannabis, tea tree, nutmeg. Less-studied terpene; included for class breadth.

name: Geraniol
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; mast-cell stabilizer in vitro
evidence_notes: Rose, palmarosa, citronella. EU fragrance allergen 26 — common contact-allergic trigger. Bidirectional in MCAS context.

name: Alpha-bisabolol
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; mast-cell stabilizer in vitro; gastric-mucosa protective
evidence_notes: Roman + German chamomile. Rare trigger; mostly anti-inflammatory profile.

name: Nerolidol
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; transdermal penetration enhancer; anti-microbial
evidence_notes: Neroli, jasmine, lemongrass. Skin-permeation enhancer property is double-edged.

name: Wogonin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer (LAD2 + RBL-2H3); Akt/MAPK suppression; Nrf2 activation; sister flavone of Baicalein
evidence_notes: Skullcap constituent. Direct in vitro inhibition of FcεRI-mediated degranulation. Lower mast-cell-affinity than baicalein but better oral bioavailability.

name: Chrysin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer in vitro; Nrf2 inducer; weak aromatase inhibitor
evidence_notes: 5,7-dihydroxyflavone. Documented inhibition of histamine release from RBL-2H3 cells. Poor oral bioavailability — formulation-sensitive.

name: Tangeretin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; NF-κB suppression; better BBB penetration than parent flavonoids
evidence_notes: 5,6,7,8,4'-pentamethoxyflavone. Methylation improves bioavailability + brain penetration. Citrus peel constituent.

name: Nobiletin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer + neuroprotective + CB1 indirect; potent anti-inflammatory
evidence_notes: Hexamethoxyflavone — even more lipophilic than tangeretin. Strong allergic-inflammation suppression in mouse models.

name: Acacetin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer in vitro; NF-κB + COX-2 suppression
evidence_notes: 4'-methoxyapigenin. Less-studied than apigenin but improved lipophilicity.

name: Myricetin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; xanthine oxidase inhibition; ROS scavenger; weak Nrf2 induction
evidence_notes: 3,3',4',5,5',7-hexahydroxyflavone — most hydroxylated common flavonol; potent antioxidant.

name: Fisetin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; senolytic (clears senescent cells); Nrf2 induction; SYK + STAT signaling suppression
evidence_notes: Quercetin's 5-deoxy analog. Senolytic activity in human trials (~2 g/day pulses). MCAS interest because senescent cells secrete SASP cytokines that could prime mast cells.

name: Galangin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; STAT6 suppression; PGE2 inhibition
evidence_notes: 3,5,7-trihydroxyflavone. Documented β-hexosaminidase inhibition in RBL-2H3 + mouse anaphylaxis.

name: Isorhamnetin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; downstream metabolite of quercetin; PPAR-γ + Nrf2
evidence_notes: 3'-O-methylquercetin. Better stability + selective tissue accumulation than quercetin.

name: Naringenin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; CYP3A4 inhibitor (grapefruit interaction); anti-inflammatory; Nrf2 induction
evidence_notes: Aglycone form of naringin (grapefruit). β-hex inhibition in mouse BMMCs. Drug-interaction caveat (CYP3A4).

name: Hesperetin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; anti-allergic in OVA-sensitized mice; weak Nrf2
evidence_notes: Aglycone of hesperidin. Better absorbed than hesperidin (which depends on gut β-glucosidase). Compare to library hesperidin.

name: Phloretin
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; GLUT1/2 inhibitor; topical applications
evidence_notes: Apple bark / peel. In vitro β-hex inhibition. Used cosmetically.

name: Xanthohumol
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; NF-κB + Nrf2 modulation; phyto-estrogen (mild)
evidence_notes: Hops constituent. Anti-inflammatory in colitis + atherosclerosis models. Estrogenic via isomerization to 8-prenylnaringenin.

name: Honokiol
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer (RBL-2H3 + mouse BMMC); SIRT3 activator; CB2 partial agonist
evidence_notes: Magnolia officinalis. Direct β-hex + TNF-α + leukotriene suppression. Crosses BBB. Some psychiatric trials.

name: Magnolol
mechanism: Mast-cell stabilizer; PPAR-γ partial agonist; GABA-A modulator
evidence_notes: Honokiol's biphenyl isomer. Often co-administered as 'honokiol/magnolol extract.'

name: Pterostilbene
mechanism: Resveratrol analog with better bioavailability; SIRT1 activator; Nrf2 induction; mast-cell stabilizer
evidence_notes: More lipophilic and longer-lived than resveratrol. Blueberry constituent.

name: Carnosic acid
mechanism: **Electrophilic catechol oxidation → covalent KEAP1 cysteine modification** (analogous to SFN mechanism but different warhead); strong Nrf2 induction
evidence_notes: Rosmarinus officinalis. Mechanistically aligned with SFN-class — covalent KEAP1 modifier via ortho-quinone intermediate after oxidation. Non-ITC structural class for KEAP1 axis.

name: Carnosol
mechanism: Oxidation product of carnosic acid; covalent KEAP1 modifier; Nrf2 induction
evidence_notes: More stable than carnosic acid. Equivalent Nrf2 induction in vitro.

name: Ursolic acid
mechanism: Nrf2 activator; NF-κB suppression; structurally related to bardoxolone class
evidence_notes: Same triterpenoid scaffold class as bardoxolone (KEAP1 modifier). Anti-inflammatory + anti-tumor evidence.

name: Oleanolic acid
mechanism: Nrf2 activator; analog of ursolic; structurally similar to bardoxolone
evidence_notes: Isomer of ursolic acid. Hepatoprotective + anti-inflammatory. Same bardoxolone-class triterpene scaffold.

name: Betulinic acid
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; Nrf2; pro-apoptotic in tumors; complement inhibition
evidence_notes: Birch + plane tree bark. Preclinical anti-inflammatory + anti-cancer.

name: Lupeol
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; mast-cell stabilizer in vitro; NF-κB suppression
evidence_notes: Widely distributed in plants. Documented β-hex inhibition in RBL-2H3.

name: Piceatannol
mechanism: **Direct SYK kinase inhibitor** (the proximal FcεRI signaling kinase in mast cells); also Nrf2 activator; resveratrol's hydroxylated metabolite
evidence_notes: Geahlen 1989 originally identified piceatannol as a SYK inhibitor — directly hits the proximal mast-cell signaling kinase. Worth highlighting because most natural products are downstream / pleiotropic; piceatannol has an actual on-pathway target.

name: Ellagic acid
mechanism: Nrf2 activator; gut-microbiome-converted to urolithins; anti-inflammatory
evidence_notes: Hydrolyzable tannin. Bioavailability depends on microbial conversion to urolithin A/B. Pomegranate ellagitannin source.

name: Cannabidiolic acid
mechanism: **Selective COX-2 inhibitor** (more potent than CBD); 5-HT1A agonist; non-intoxicating
evidence_notes: Raw cannabis CBDA — decarboxylates to CBD with heat. Takeda 2008 showed COX-2 selectivity comparable to selective NSAIDs. Mast-cell-relevant via prostaglandin axis.

name: Tetrahydrocannabinolic acid
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; non-intoxicating (does not cross BBB); decarboxylates to THC with heat
evidence_notes: Raw cannabis THCA. Preserved by minimizing heat exposure. Anti-emetic + neuroprotective preclinical.

name: Cannabigerolic acid
mechanism: PPAR-α agonist; precursor to CBGA→CBG/THCA/CBDA; SARS-CoV-2 spike binding (preclinical)
evidence_notes: Biosynthetic precursor to all other phytocannabinoid acids. Less direct mast-cell data than its descendants.

name: Cannabicyclol
mechanism: Minor cannabinoid; very limited pharmacology
evidence_notes: Forms from CBC under UV exposure. Almost no in vivo data; included for structural-class breadth.

name: Cannabichromenic acid
subcategory: Phytocannabinoid acid (raw CBC precursor — PubChem CID may resolve to CBC)
mechanism: Anti-microbial; precursor to CBC
evidence_notes: CBCA — note: some databases list this CID under CBC (decarboxylated form). Pipeline will use whatever PubChem returns.
